patch.value:
## Context
Windows-specific handling found in tests:
- tests/unit/test-all-command.spec.ts line 896: extended timeout on win32
- tests/unit/test-command.spec.ts line 1820: signal fixture skip on win32
- tests/unit/vector-store-adapter.spec.ts line 663: itOnPosix skip on win32

But: fs-utils.spec.ts and store-paths.spec.ts have no win32-conditional tests.

Nightly actually runs full test suite on windows-latest (not just smoke), so any path-separator bugs would surface there, but are not proactively tested in unit coverage.

## Acceptance criteria
- fs-utils.spec.ts has at least one test verifying path construction on simulated win32 (or a comment explaining the platform is safe)
- store-paths.spec.ts verifies path outputs are platform-appropriate
- OR: a review of fs-utils.ts and paths.ts confirms path.join() is used throughout with no hardcoded separators

## Evidence
tests/unit/fs-utils.spec.ts; tests/unit/store-paths.spec.ts; tests/unit/test-all-command.spec.ts line 896; nightly.yml windows-latest entry
Add Windows path-separator test coverage for fs-utils and store/paths

The nightly CI matrix includes windows-latest/Node20, and tests/unit/ has some win32-conditional logic (test-all-command.spec.ts line 896, test-command.spec.ts line 1820, vector-store-adapter.spec.ts line 663). However fs-utils.spec.ts and store-paths.spec.ts do not have Windows-specific path-separator coverage. Path construction using path.join() is safe, but hardcoded forward-slash usage in paths (e.g. .agents/pm/ pattern matching) could break on Windows.

- fs-utils.spec.ts has at least one test verifying path construction on simulated win32 (or a comment explaining the platform is safe)
- store-paths.spec.ts verifies path outputs are platform-appropriate
- OR: a review of fs-utils.ts and paths.ts confirms path.join() is used throughout with no hardcoded separators

value.author: codex-implementation-20260619
value.text: Evidence: added path coverage for fs-utils atomic writes preserving platform path semantics and store path format parsing for Windows-style tracker paths. Verification passed: linked fs-utils/store-paths test, pnpm typecheck, pnpm quality:static, pnpm changelog:pm:check unchanged with pm-changelog 2026.6.14, node scripts/run-tests.mjs coverage = 100/100/100/100 (4655 tests), pm validate history drift ok with pre-existing missing-resolution warning backlog, and isolated temp-dir manual smoke passed.

author: codex-implementation-20260619

patch.value: Added focused cross-platform path tests for atomic file writes and Windows-style tracker path item-format parsing.
Path helper behavior has proactive coverage for platform path construction and Windows-style tracker paths.
fs-utils and store-paths focused tests now cover platform path semantics and Windows-style .agents\pm item paths; linked tests pass.

message: Record resolution for Windows path coverage

patch.value: Windows path coverage implemented and verified: linked focused tests pass, full coverage is 100/100/100/100, static/typecheck/changelog/validate/manual temp smoke passed.

value.author: codex-implementation-20260619
value.text: Review follow-up: addressed Gemini feedback on fs-utils path test by reloading fs-utils with a mocked node:fs/promises.writeFile and asserting the atomic temp path is created in the target directory. Verification: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/core/fs/fs-utils.spec.ts tests/unit/core/store/store-paths.spec.ts passed; ci-workflow-contract focused test also passed.

value.author: codex
value.text: Review follow-up: Gemini flagged POSIX parsing fragility for Windows-style tracker paths with dotted directory names; normalizing separator handling in getItemFormatFromPath and adding negative coverage before merge.
